title: Fluffy MiddleEastern Lamb Tagine
description: Herbal, fluffy lamb tagine with a crispy coating and rich, reduced sauce, featuring fajita seasoning, dairyfree and glutenfree for a showoff Middle Eastern dinner.
instructions: Pat lamb cubes dry and season with salt and pepper.
Whisk chickpea flour with fajita seasoning, cumin, coriander, and cardamom in a bowl. Toss lamb until evenly coated.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet or tagine over medium‑high heat. Brown lamb on all sides, about 8‑10 minutes. Transfer browned lamb to a slow cooker.
In the same skillet, sauté onion until translucent, about 4 minutes. Add garlic and cook for another 30 seconds.
Add the onion mixture, tomatoes, and chicken broth to the slow cooker. Stir to combine and ensure the lamb is mostly submerged.
Cover and cook on low for 4–5 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the sauce has reduced to a thick, fluffy consistency.
About 30 minutes before serving, stir in the chopped fresh herbs.
Serve hot over couscous or flatbread, garnished with extra herbs if desired.
